title: Coelogyne albiflora
taxonomy: Coelogyne albiflora is a species of orchid. It is classified under the family Orchidaceae, which includes over 30,000 species of orchids. This species belongs to the genus Coelogyne, a group of orchids known for their white or yellowish flowers. Coelogyne albiflora is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large subfamily of orchids. The classification of Coelogyne albiflora is also supported by molecular studies that have confirmed its place within the Orchidaceae family.

intro: Coelogyne albiflora is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its white flowers and ability to thrive in shaded, humid environments. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne albiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne albiflora is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance their therapeutic effects.
